Title: Politicians and the public agree on what the Spending Review’s priorities should be
Description: With the UK Government’s spending review due to conclude in June 2025, Mitya Pearson and David Jeffery argue that while politicians and the public mostly agree on public spending priorities, politicians need to make the case to the public where they do not.

Title: Is the UK-EU reset the first step toward Britain rejoining the single market?
Description: The UK and the EU have reached agreement on a post-Brexit “reset” to their relationship. Mitya Pearson and David Jeffery write that while there is support in the UK for establishing closer relations with the EU, there are major political obstacles to taking more ambitious steps like rejoining the single market.

Title: What do MPs really think about immigration? We surveyed them to find out
Description: Members of the public are more likely than MPs to think immigration has been too high in recent years.

Title: It was Thatcher wot lost it – or was it? Conservative electoral decline in Liverpool since 1945
Description: Contrary to popular narratives that see Margaret Thatcher as the cause of Conservative decline in Liverpool, David Jeffery explains that various other factors were in play, long before Thatcher came to power. Those factors, combined with the rise of the Liberals in the 1970s, displaced the Conservatives as the main local opposition to Labour.
